issues: base green is dark/saturated rather than bright-warm — reduces toy-diorama luminosity
curved flower cluster arrangement creates a visible diagonal sweep when tiled — breaks seamless illusion at macro scale
reasoning: Warm flower palette (pink/white/orange accents on deep green) correctly invokes Zelda outdoor flora and shows good emotional intent. Pixel placement is deliberate and chunky: flower patches read as hard-edged 2–4px clusters with no gradient bleed or noise mush, and the tile seams are invisible at 1x. Technically competent. Two concerns hold it from a pass: (1) the base green is rich and dark — closer to forest shadow than the bright, sun-lit Hyrule ground the target aesthetic calls for; (2) the scattered flowers follow a subtle curved sweep arrangement that becomes a repeating diagonal streak across a 2×2 tile grid, making the tiling pattern perceptible. Emotional score ~6, technical ~7; weighted 60/40 → 6.
